title: CXXRTL: assert on nets with multiple drivers
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

[endless_toggle.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/5177255/endless_toggle.zip)

Verilog design with combinational feedback loop:

```verilog
module endless_toggle(input clk1);

    wire toggle = 0;
    wire taggle;

    assign taggle = ~toggle;
    assign toggle = taggle;

endmodule
```

```
yosys -p "read_verilog endless_toggle.v; hierarchy -top endless_toggle; write_cxxrtl endless_toggle.cpp"
```

```
 Yosys 0.9+3565 (git sha1 474cd02e, clang 11.0.0 -fPIC -Os)
```
## Expected behavior

CXXRTL does something that doesn't crash. Error message or C code.

## Actual behavior

```
ERROR: Assert `flow.wire_comb_defs[wire].size() == 1' failed in backends/cxxrtl/cxxrtl_backend.cc:2185.
```

It's obviously a nonsense design, but chances are that this assert would trigger valid designs as well.
code_blocks: yosys -p "read_verilog endless_toggle.v; hierarchy -top endless_toggle; write_cxxrtl endless_toggle.cpp"
